speaker: Narrator
text:
The sidewalks are packed with adults and children bustling in this nice springtime weather. However, here walks Christina, a shambling mess compared to everyone else around her. 
	
	Christina's paranoia starts to kick in. They all must keenly aware of her presence, she knows it.
	
	She sees young men shooting glances at her midriff. She sees the kids walking around her at the smell of her. She keeps her head down and speedwalks towards her destination.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Max was like the Swedish equivalent to Mcdonald's or Burger King. They almost shut down last year from severe food safety violations, but somehow they recovered from the scandal. 
	
	That's not quite the reason why she's avoided coming here though, She once had a crush on one of the cashiers that worked there. 
	
	It was like an angel was smiling brightly as Christina walked through those front doors. She had dark brunette hair tied up and stuffed with a bun underneath her cap, truly a beauty.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina becomes flustered from her gaze and she rushes over to the self service terminals away from sight. Unfortunately, she found them all of them were found to be busted.

	She's front to confront the cashier at the desk.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina straightens her back in a vain attempt to mimic normal human posture but instead resembles an octopus fresh out of water. 
	
	She looks to her right, to avoid eye contact with the cashier.

speaker: Girl
text: Hi and welcome to Max, what would you like to order? Oh, it's you!

speaker: Girl
text: Welcome back. I think I've seen you around before!

speaker: Christina
text: Hahah, oh uh... thanksyoutoo!

speaker: Girl
text: What?

speaker: Christina
text: Ahem, I mean... J-just get me the uh... Cheeseburger combo meal?

speaker: Christina
text: Make it t-takeaway.

speaker: Narrator
text: The cashier girl catches a whiff of a strange scent. It's something emanating from Christina, and it's overwhelming her senses. If endearment was an aroma, this would be it.

speaker: Girl
text: That'll be...

speaker: Girl
text: [wave]Uh, Are you wearing a fragrance by the way?[/wave]

speaker: Christina
text: No? Um, how much is the food?

speaker: Girl
text: Sorry, it's 150 kronor.

speaker: Christina
text: Alright, here it is.

speaker: Girl
text: Could you stay for a second?

speaker: Narrator
text: She takes a pen out of her pocket and scribbles something on her receipt.

speaker: Girl
text: This is for you.

speaker: Narrator
text: When Christina reaches for the receipt, the girl suddenly latches onto her hand and gently caresses her thumb.

speaker: Christina
text: Hey, let go of my hand!

speaker: Blanche
text: [wave]It's not your body, it's your mouth.[/wave]

speaker: Girl
text: [wave]What's inside you?[/wave]

speaker: Girl
text: [wave]I wonder what your other parts smell like~[/wave]

speaker: Christina
text: Let go!

speaker: Girl
text: [shake]It's so sweet.[/shake]

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina grits her teeth and shoves the cashier's arm away with all her might. She just barely backs off panting before she could grab her again. 
	
	Christina realizes in horror that she's meanmugging her crush and slows her breathing. She looks around the restaurant to see the strange looks being are giving. 
	
	The cashier seems to have snapped back to reality, they both don't say anything. It's too awkward to. She shuffles to the side and waits for her order to be complete. 
	
	She takes the time to unfold for her receipt, it's the cashier's number. Christina pressess her nose against the receipt and sniffs. It smells like peach hard candies.

speaker: Narrator
text: Before long, her order is finished being prepared. Luckily or unluckily, a different employee hands the bag to Christina instead of her crush.
